problem: Local service businesses publish inconsistent, generic media and advertising without an affordable expert who can audit the whole presence and turn findings into usable improvements.
target_user: Owner or marketing operator of a German local service business
one_sentence_value: Visibel turns one business profile into an evidence-backed media audit, prioritized fixes, and an improved cross-channel preview in under two minutes.
input: Google Business and Instagram URLs when available
happy_path: Create a LIVE or visibly labelled REPLAY audit
Run research, screenshot, vision, scoring, and recommendation stages with persisted progress
Inspect the score, findings, evidence, and prioritized recommendations
Improve all or selected channels and compare the optimized preview
wow_moment: A weak, fragmented local-business presence becomes a concrete, evidence-linked cross-channel campaign preview while the score visibly improves.
business_metric: Time from input to actionable audit
Number of recommendations accepted for improvement

evidence_policy.supporting_artifact_rule: Plans, contracts, reports, and handoffs support this revision but never replace the four authoritative Run artifacts.

judging_map.criterion: Innovation and agentic use of partner technology
judging_map.visible_evidence: Five named pipeline stages, truthful provider provenance, and ordered progress produce one scored audit.
Prioritized findings lead directly to channel-specific improved assets and a score delta.
Persisted audit history, evidence links, image assets, deterministic replay, and fallback remain usable end to end.

experience_contract.ten_second_takeaway: See what customers see, fix what costs trust, and compare the result in one clear flow.
experience_contract.visual_direction: Use the selected Luminous Focus composition with premium editorial restraint: near-black ink, warm white paper, muted stone neutrals, generous whitespace, and large confident type. Blue, purple, synthetic glows, generic gradient effects, and dense microcopy are excluded.
experience_contract.hero_interaction: Enter the business, run the audit, inspect the prioritized result, then improve the presence and compare the preview.

experience_foundation.selected_foundation: The human-selected 01-luminous-focus.html composition, implemented through the existing Next.js 15 shell and local primitives
experience_foundation.selection_rationale: The reference supplies the approved hierarchy and spacious composition. Existing components preserve working application behavior while the new token system removes blue-purple AI styling and reduces low-value microcopy.

component_reuse_plan: Reuse the existing application shell, form logic, report cards, preview components, polling, downloads, uploads, and navigation
Restyle the shared primitives and route shells with the frozen monochrome editorial tokens
Reuse the frozen Zod schemas as the frontend/backend boundary
Keep GET polling as a fallback when SSE is unavailable

provider_release_contract.rationale: OpenAI, Tavily, and Cognee are partner capabilities required by the approved concept; the runnable proof is local and falls back to a visibly labelled replay when credentials or venue connectivity are unavailable.
provider_release_contract.not_applicable_rule: Not applicable because external capability is part of the approved hero; public hosting remains a human-authorized handoff.
live_mode_requires: Provider credentials supplied outside the repository
Passing local health and primary-operation smoke tests
Explicit human approval before any public deployment or organizer submission

release_target.provider: Local Next.js process; public host HANDOFF_REQUIRED

tech_blueprint_contract.rule: This completion Run adopts the already human-selected implementation plan and frozen schemas; breaking contract changes require a new revision.

stack_recipe.frontend: Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
stack_recipe.backend: Next.js route handlers and a staged TypeScript audit pipeline
stack_recipe.persistence: better-sqlite3 with deterministic fixture-derived replay records
stack_recipe.deployment: Local Node.js process for the event; public deployment requires explicit human authorization

persistence.rationale: Audit history, stage progress, reports, evidence, memory context, and generated assets must survive route requests and support replay.
persistence.seed_strategy: Replay and rehearsal records derive from fixture.json and the frozen sample audit.

repository_layout: code/media-ad-coach/lib — schemas, persistence, providers, pipeline, and improvement logic
code/media-ad-coach/tests — focused contract and integration tests
parallel_build_order: Preserve the frozen schema and shared package surfaces
Mission Control owns the human-authorized frontend refresh as one serialized implementation task
Backend Integrator owns non-overlapping screenshot, streaming, image-edit, memory, and API paths
Mission Control integrates and Reviewer independently verifies

feature_budget.admission_rule: Only approved features that improve judging evidence, the hero interaction, or demo recovery are admitted; the frontend refresh must not alter frozen schemas or provider semantics.

features.judging_contribution: Completes real screenshot evidence, image edit, SSE progress, and truthful Cognee interoperability.
features.visible_payoff: The existing frontend can show real progress and evidence while retaining replay fallback.
features.acceptance_command: pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm test && pnpm build

features.judging_contribution: Produces repeatable seeded audits, Wi-Fi-off replay rehearsals, and a truthful delivery handoff.
features.visible_payoff: Judges can see the complete product even when venue providers are unavailable.

features.judging_contribution: Makes the complete product immediately legible and visually distinctive across the input, report, history, and preview flow.
features.visible_payoff: A premium monochrome editorial interface with concise copy, responsive layouts, and all existing actions intact.
features.acceptance_command: pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm test && pnpm build

required_capabilities.name: Evidence-backed staged media audit
required_capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Persisted stage progress, report evidence, and score are returned by the audit routes in LIVE or labelled REPLAY mode.

required_capabilities.name: Channel copy and image improvement
required_capabilities.acceptance_evidence: Selected recommendations create persisted improved assets and an optimized preview, capped at three image operations.

required_capabilities.name: Deterministic replay and provider fallback
required_capabilities.acceptance_evidence: The same frozen sample completes with a visible REPLAY label and no live-provider claim.

must: MUST-001: Complete an evidence-backed audit in LIVE or visibly labelled REPLAY mode.
MUST-002: Turn prioritized recommendations into judge-visible improved channel assets.
MUST-003: Persist audits, progress, assets, provenance, and recovery state.
MUST-004: Implement the human-approved frontend refresh without changing the frozen UI/API schema or provider semantics.
wont: Do not change frozen schemas, API semantics, persistence, or provider responsibilities as part of the visual refresh.
Do not start P2 F-110/F-111 before P0 and P1 are green.
Do not provision, spend, publish, or submit without explicit human authorization.
Do not present replay, missing credentials, or failed providers as LIVE.
acceptance: All admitted code paths pass typecheck, focused tests, and production build.
The full input-to-report-to-preview flow remains usable at 1440x900 and a narrow mobile viewport.
The visible interface uses no blue or purple styling, avoids synthetic glow effects, and keeps primary explanatory copy at a readable size.
Replay completes without network access and remains visibly labelled.
The frozen GET polling route remains compatible while SSE is additive.
No secrets are committed or sent to the client.
Tracker status distinguishes implemented work from venue-only and human-only handoffs.
